summary:
Self-reported health-related quality of life (HRQL), particularly composite scores, has been shown to be an independent prognostic factor for survival in non-small-cell lung cancer patients, remaining significant even after adjustment for standard covariates such as performance status and tumor staging. It reflects more than just symptom presence; it offers clinically meaningful insights into long-term prognosis by capturing overall functional well-being.
